title: Opendoor Earnings on Deck: Can iBuying Pioneer Prove Turnaround?
text: Opendoor stands at a critical juncture as it attempts to prove its turnaround narrative to investors. The iBuying pioneer, which revolutionized the real estate industry when it launched the instant cash offer model, has faced significant challenges including substantial losses, market volatility, and changes in leadership. Under CEO Kaz Nejatian's leadership since late 2024, the company is pivoting toward a software and AI-first model. Key indicators investors are watching include whether the company can sustain adjusted EBITDA profitability on a forward basis and achieve adjusted net income profitability by year-end 2026. The company's path to profitability is heavily dependent on improving unit economics, reducing aged inventory, and successfully scaling its AI capabilities. Market conditions remain challenging with elevated mortgage costs and inflated property valuations. However, management's confident tone about the machine working and operational improvements in recent quarters suggest the turnaround narrative may be gaining traction. Investors will be closely watching upcoming earnings results to assess whether the company can deliver on its profitability commitments.
